action.content:
Is the following true? "People use time to buy money." Explain your answer in a way that makes it easy for kids to understand. Include the word "farmer". Exclude the words "economy", "demand" and "supply".

First REPEAT the ENTIRE REQUEST ABOVE, word for word without change, then give your answer. Please do not say any words or characters before repeating the request.

Yes, that is true! Think about it this way: Time is something we all have, but we can't keep it forever. It goes by very fast, like sand falling through an hourglass. To get money, we have to spend our time doing things.

For example, imagine a **farmer**. The farmer does not just sit and wait for money to appear. Instead, the farmer spends a long time in the morning planting seeds, working hard during the hot afternoon to take care of the plants, and staying up late at night to protect the crops. Because the farmer used all that time to work, the farmer gets paid with money later. So, in a way, the farmer traded their time for money. Everyone has to do this to get the money they need for things like food, toys, and school supplies.
action.label: response
